discovery.run_summary: Mixed tape with semis (SMH -2.6%) and large-cap tech weighing on QQQ while defensives (XLV +2%, XLP +1.3%) and financials lead. Iran/oil backdrop continues, and Trump's China trip is on deck. Mid-cap movers are concentrated in tech/semi equipment names dragged by the SMH selloff.

notable_index_moves: SMH -2.61% — semis under heavy pressure, ORCL -3.6% and AVGO -2.1% leading mega-cap weakness
XLV +1.96% defensive bid despite FDA commissioner resignation headline
VIX -2% to 18 — risk-off in tech but not broad-based panic
Russell (IWM) -0.96% suggests mid/small-cap pressure broader than just semis

discoveries.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc
discoveries.setup: Semi-cap-equipment sympathy selloff layered on top of a mild governance 8-K from 4 days ago
discoveries.thesis: AEIS down 4.4% on below-average volume aligns more with SMH -2.6% sympathy than with the May 8 8-K, which contained 5.02/5.03/5.07/9.01 — the combination strongly suggests this was a routine annual-meeting filing (5.07 is shareholder vote results, 5.03 is charter amendment, 5.02 here likely director elections, 9.01 boilerplate), not a sudden departure. If the 8-K were materially bad it would have hit on May 8, not 4 days later on light volume. The semi-equipment beta drag explains most of today's move; magnitude looks extended versus the actual catalyst chain.
discoveries.what_confirms: If SMH stabilizes and AEIS reverses faster than peers (LRCX, AMAT, ACLS) over next 1-3 sessions; if reading the 8-K exhibits confirms 5.02 was routine director election, not an executive departure.
discoveries.what_kills: If the 5.02 item actually documents a CFO/CEO departure with no successor named, the governance overhang is real and -4% is justified or insufficient; if SMH keeps falling, sympathy drag continues regardless of AEIS fundamentals.
discoveries.what_to_learn: 5.02 + 5.03 + 5.07 + 9.01 in a single 8-K dated near the typical annual-meeting window is almost always proxy-vote results, not a negative governance event — distinguish from standalone 5.02 filings which carry real signal.
discoveries.catalyst: Semi-cap sympathy with SMH -2.6%; May 8 8-K likely annual meeting results
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Combination of 5.02+5.03+5.07 items is the signature of an annual shareholder meeting filing, not a bad-news bundle; today's move on sub-average volume suggests sector beta, not company news.
research_pointers: Open the 8-K exhibits and verify 5.02 is director election results vs an unplanned departure
Compare AEIS intraday move to LRCX, AMAT, KLAC, ACLS to size the pure semi-beta component
Check if AEIS has any data-center/AI power exposure narrative that could explain divergence

discoveries.name: Extreme Networks, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Networking small-cap down hard on below-average volume with no SEC filing or earnings catalyst visible
discoveries.thesis: No 8-K, no recent or upcoming earnings flagged, volume actually below average — a 6%+ drop on 0.7x volume is unusual and suggests either (a) a single seller / illiquidity, (b) sympathy with a peer guide-down not in our feed, or (c) something specific not yet showing in filings. Cannot articulate a directional thesis at conf 3+ without identifying the driver.
discoveries.what_confirms: Discovery of a peer (CSCO, ANET, JNPR, HPE networking) guidance event or a sell-side downgrade specific to EXTR.
discoveries.what_kills: If price recovers tomorrow on no news, confirms today was thin-tape noise; if an 8-K appears overnight, the catalyst was simply not yet filed when we looked.
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — no visible filings or earnings catalyst
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals is empty; volume is sub-average, which argues against a broadly-known news catalyst.
research_pointers: Check for analyst downgrade or peer (ANET, JNPR, HPE) commentary today
Watch for any after-hours 8-K filing tonight
Review options activity for unusual pre-positioning

discoveries.name: Photronics, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Small-cap photomask supplier moving with the semi-equipment selloff
discoveries.thesis: PLAB -5.4% with SMH -2.6% is roughly the expected beta for a small-cap semi-supply-chain name on a sharp semi down day. Slightly elevated volume (1.25x) but no catalyst_signals — most likely pure sector sympathy. Magnitude is in line with what high-beta semi small caps typically do on a 2.5%+ SMH down move.
discoveries.what_confirms: If SMH recovery is met with proportional PLAB recovery; peer photomask/wafer-supply names (e.g., ENTG, MKSI) moving similarly today.
discoveries.what_kills: Discovery of a PLAB-specific catalyst (customer concentration warning, China export-control news given Trump's China trip context) would reclassify this as something other than rational sympathy.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals plus a move consistent with high-beta semi-supplier behavior on a SMH -2.6% day.
research_pointers: Cross-check with photomask peers and any China export-control headlines tied to Trump's Beijing trip
Monitor whether PLAB beta to SMH has changed structurally

catalyst_chains.primary_event: SMH -2.6%, ORCL -3.6%, AVGO -2.1% — broad semi/AI infra selloff
Trump China trip imminent; auto-state lawmakers pushing Chinese-vehicle ban; Huang notably NOT on the trip

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump heading to China, friendly tone toward Xi; separately offers to talk with Cuba
trump_signals.note: Conciliatory China rhetoric could be tailwind for semi-equipment names with China exposure, but Huang's absence from the delegation and lawmakers' anti-China-vehicle bill cut the other way. Today's semi selloff may partly reflect uncertainty about what concessions emerge from the trip on chip export controls.

discovery.no_signals_note: Mover set is small (3 names) and dominated by semi-cap sympathy moves; the AEIS 8-K is the only company-specific item and likely benign on closer inspection. Light catalyst night overall.

ai_announcements.headline: Anthropic enters AI legal services market
ai_announcements.summary: Anthropic launching tools for law firms: document search/review, case law research, deposition prep, document drafting.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Public legal tech names (e.g., RELX/LexisNexis parent, Thomson Reuters TRI) have deep proprietary case law databases and enterprise law firm relationships; Anthropic likely partners or licenses rather than fully displaces. No public pure-play 'AI legal' stock moved meaningfully today.
research_pointers: Check TRI and RELX intraday reaction
Anthropic-related call: flag for bias review
Watch Harvey AI / vLex competitive positioning commentary

ai_announcements.headline: Google adds Gemini-powered dictation to Gboard
ai_announcements.summary: Native Gemini dictation/transcription on Pixel and Samsung Galaxy phones threatens standalone dictation/transcription startups.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Main affected players (Otter, Rev) are private. Public adjacents like NUAN (acquired by MSFT) not tradeable. No clear public victim today.

ai_announcements.headline: Google/SpaceX in talks for orbital data centers
ai_announcements.summary: Speculative, multi-year. Could pressure terrestrial DC infra narrative long-term but not near-term.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: The three stocks flagged in market_data (PLAB -5.4%, EXTR -6.3%, AEIS -4.4%) do not have a clear linkage to today's AI announcements. PLAB (photomask maker) and AEIS (power conversion for semicap) are semicap-adjacent and likely moving on sector/semi-equipment dynamics, not on Google Android announcements or Anthropic legal launch. EXTR (enterprise networking) could have tangential exposure to AI-driven networking spend shifts but no specific catalyst in today's news; AEIS also had a recent 8-K with officer departure (5.02) on 5/8 which is a more likely idiosyncratic driver than AI news. Recommend Michael NOT trade these as AI-reaction names without further catalyst confirmation.
